# Questions in Livestream 72

Live Stream #72: The Origin of All Knowledge

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d837zh26DZw?t=763" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:43</a> | Is there an etymological connection between the Egyptian god Atum and Adam, and can `atum` mean red? | No. He said the words are not etymologically related, and Egyptian `tm` refers to totality or completeness, not redness. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d837zh26DZw?t=1125" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">18:45</a> | Did ancient Egyptian omit the copular verb, and what about Greek? | He said Middle Egyptian often omits the copula, while later Egyptian uses it more regularly; Koine Greek, by contrast, normally requires a verb. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d837zh26DZw?t=1381" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">23:01</a> | How does Zoroastrianism fit into the development of gnosticism? | He did not think Zoroastrianism directly explains gnosticism and argued that gnosticism is not truly simple dualism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d837zh26DZw?t=1824" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:24</a> | Is there a credible source table for the chronology of Judges, since the numbers do not add up sequentially? | He recommended Kenneth Kitchen's treatment in *On the Reliability of the Old Testament* and said the judges must overlap in concurrent timelines rather than run sequentially. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d837zh26DZw?t=2158" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">35:58</a> | Who are the ministering women with the mirrors in Exodus 38:8, and are the mirrors tied to Hathor worship? | He said the women are not fully identified in the text, but Egyptian mirrors commonly did carry Hathor imagery, so a Hathoric association is plausible. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d837zh26DZw?t=2618" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">43:38</a> | Could the Merneptah Stele refer to Israel still in Egypt or to Israelite tribes already in Canaan before the Exodus? | He rejected both options, arguing that the stele works better as a reference to Israel in Canaan after a real Exodus. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d837zh26DZw?t=3118" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">51:58</a> | How should Noah's family be understood from a clan viewpoint, and could others from those clans have been spared? | He said Noah's sons can be read as clan leaders and that, while speculative, the eight people in the ark might be an inclusive count of key members rather than necessarily excluding all others. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d837zh26DZw?t=3428" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">57:08</a> | Could Mesopotamian stories have been imported into Egypt during the Late Bronze Age? | Yes. He said texts and ideas moved widely across the ancient Near East, so such transmission into Egypt is entirely conceivable. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d837zh26DZw?t=3691" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:01:31</a> | If ancient peoples used 360-day or 365-day years, did they adjust their calendars to keep them aligned with the seasons? | He said Egypt used a 365-day year without intercalation, and before the seventh century BC there is no clear evidence that Israel or the Levant regularly aligned their calendars with the seasons. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d837zh26DZw?t=4023" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:07:03</a> | How can Goliath's sword be in Ahimelech's possession in 1 Samuel 21 if David took it to his tent in 1 Samuel 17? | He explained that David could have taken the weapons initially, but they likely became national-religious trophies kept at Nob as symbols of Yahweh's victory. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d837zh26DZw?t=4752" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:19:12</a> | Why did Pharaoh not simply kill Moses once it was clear Moses would keep asking for Israel's release? | He argued Pharaoh's power was constrained by Egyptian legal and political tradition, and killing Moses without sufficient cause would have been dangerous for a Ramesside king. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d837zh26DZw?t=5226" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:27:06</a> | What is your scholarly opinion of Tell el-Hammam as a candidate for Sodom or Gomorrah? | He rejected the identification, citing problems with location, dating, stratigraphy, unpublished raw data, and weak support for the proposed destruction scenario. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d837zh26DZw?t=5884" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:38:04</a> | Is the Book of Jonah satire? | He said no, though he acknowledged strong irony and humor in the book. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d837zh26DZw?t=6211" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:43:31</a> | What advice would you give for teaching a church class on the heresy of Marcionism? | He said not to confuse Marcionism with gnosticism, to explain its two-god framework, and to connect it to modern practical neglect of the Old Testament. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d837zh26DZw?t=6641" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:50:41</a> | Do you have an opinion on possible gnostic influence on political philosophers such as Hegel and Marx? | He said no, because political philosophy is outside his usual reading and he was not familiar enough with Hegel or Marx to comment responsibly. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d837zh26DZw?t=7131" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:58:51</a> | Should `ruach` in Genesis 1 be translated as wind rather than spirit, especially in view of Genesis 8:1? | He thought "Spirit of God" fits Genesis 1 better than "wind," while acknowledging that `ruach` has a broad semantic range and alternative translations need contextual justification. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d837zh26DZw?t=7808" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:10:08</a> | Where was the Messiah born if the answer is not simply Bethlehem: a shepherd's cave, a courtyard, or a peasant home's shelter? | He argued for a lean-to or hay-storage shelter associated with a four-room Semitic house, not a cave or the crowded courtyard area. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d837zh26DZw?t=8184" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:16:24</a> | Was `Malachi` a title like `my messenger`, or was it a personal name? | He said either is possible and the evidence is not firm enough to decide. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d837zh26DZw?t=8320" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:18:40</a> | What should be made of Bart Ehrman's claim that Moses did not exist, while some critical Egyptologists with faith commitments think he did? | He said denying Moses can itself be a faith commitment and argued that critical Egyptologists are the relevant experts when assessing evidence for the Exodus and Moses. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d837zh26DZw?t=9254" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:34:14</a> | Could the `Yabin` in Ramesses II's topographical list be the Jabin of Hazor? | Possibly, but he said he would need closer work on the list and the etymology before speaking confidently. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d837zh26DZw?t=9594" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:39:54</a> | Are there ancient sources showing that long ages, like those in Genesis, could refer to clan life rather than individual lifespans? | He pointed to very long reigns or ages in Mesopotamian and early Egyptian king lists as possible analogies, while noting that clan structures are clearest in cultures with nomadic roots and written preservation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d837zh26DZw?t=9961" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:46:01</a> | Was manna real, and if so what might it have been? | He said yes, and suggested a fungus or mushroom is a more plausible explanation than tree sap. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d837zh26DZw?t=10477" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:54:37</a> | Why did Egypt not create overseas colonies like Phoenicia? | He said Egypt generally did not need them for trade, though he noted Byblos as an exceptional Egyptian colonial outpost. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/d837zh26DZw?t=11007" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:03:27</a> | What is a good overview book on Egyptian mythology and its development? | He recommended Wilkinson's *Gods and Goddesses of Ancient Egypt*. |
